description: Fixed garden method definitions for SSA (planning + sessions). Includes indigenous/culture-specific, natural, and modern methods. Each method provides default steps, cues, timers, safety notes, and variants.

notes: Steps are designed to be runnable by SSA SessionRunner.
Where seasonal timing matters, methods include seasonHints and climateNotes; users/households can override via layer_overrides.

methods.name: Basic Soil Test + Amendment Plan
methods.summary: Collect representative soil samples, run a basic pH/NPK test, and create a simple amendment plan for the bed/plot.
climateNotes: If soil is frozen or saturated, delay sampling until workable.

supplies.name: Soil test kit or lab submission bag

safety: Wear gloves; wash hands after handling soil.

steps.title: Choose sampling zones
steps.body: Divide the garden into zones (beds/plots). Avoid atypical spots (compost pile edge, pet areas). Mark each zone name in SSA.
cues: You have a list of zones/beds you want to improve.

steps.title: Collect subsamples
steps.body: From each zone, take 8–12 small subsamples (4–6 inches deep). Combine in the bucket for that zone.
cues: Soil depth is consistent; debris removed.

steps.title: Prepare the sample
steps.body: Remove rocks/roots. Mix well. Air-dry if needed (no heat).
cues: Sample looks uniform and crumbly.

steps.title: Test / submit
steps.body: Run the kit or package for lab submission. Record results per zone in SSA.
cues: pH and nutrients recorded for each zone.

steps.title: Create an amendment plan
steps.body: Based on results, list amendments (lime/sulfur/compost/minerals) with rates per area. Save as a plan linked to the zone.
cues: Plan includes what, how much, and when to apply.

variants.name: Lab Soil Test
notes: Use a local extension/lab for more complete results (CEC, micros). Add the report PDF as an artifact.

methods.name: Hot Compost (Fast, High-Heat)
methods.summary: Build and manage a hot compost pile to rapidly convert greens/browns into finished compost.

steps.title: Choose location and base
steps.body: Pick a well-drained spot. Start on bare soil if possible for drainage and microbes.

steps.title: Layer to target ratio
steps.body: Aim for ~2–3 parts browns to 1 part greens by volume. Build at least 3x3x3 ft for heat.
cues: Pile reaches at least 1 meter wide/tall for heat retention.

steps.title: Moisten to 'wrung sponge'
steps.body: Water as you build. Material should feel like a wrung-out sponge—moist but not dripping.
cues: No puddles; handful squeezed releases only a few drops.

steps.title: Monitor heat
steps.body: Check temperature daily if using a thermometer. Turn when it peaks then begins dropping.
cues: Center is warm/hot; steam may appear when turned.

steps.title: Turn the pile
steps.body: Turn thoroughly, moving outer material to the center. Add water if dry.

steps.title: Cure and screen
steps.body: When pile no longer heats and materials are crumbly/dark, let cure 2–4 weeks. Screen if desired.
cues: Finished compost smells earthy; no recognizable food scraps.

variants.name: 18-Day Hot Compost (Berkeley)
notes: Turn every 2 days after initial 3–4 days of heating; keep moisture consistent.

methods.name: Cold Compost (Low Effort)
methods.summary: Slow composting method: add materials over time and let them break down with minimal turning.

steps.title: Set up the bin/pile
steps.body: Use a bin, pallet enclosure, or simple pile. Ensure airflow and drainage.

steps.title: Add materials as available
steps.body: Alternate greens and browns. Cover fresh scraps with browns to reduce odor and pests.
cues: No exposed food scraps on top.

steps.title: Moisture check monthly
steps.body: If dry, water lightly. If soggy, add browns and fluff.

steps.title: Optional turn
steps.body: Turn 1–3 times per season if you want faster breakdown.

steps.title: Harvest finished compost
steps.body: When crumbly and earthy-smelling, harvest from the bottom/oldest section.
cues: Dark, uniform, no slime, no strong odor.

methods.name: Vermicomposting (Worm Bin)
methods.summary: Use composting worms to convert kitchen scraps into worm castings for seed starting and beds.
safety: Avoid adding oily foods; keep bin aerated to prevent smells.

steps.title: Prepare bedding
steps.body: Moisten bedding to wrung-sponge texture. Fill bin 2/3 full.
cues: Bedding is damp, not dripping.

steps.title: Add worms and settle
steps.body: Add worms, cover with bedding. Let them settle 24 hours before feeding heavily.

steps.title: Feed lightly and consistently
steps.body: Bury scraps in rotating corners. Add crushed eggshells for grit if desired.
cues: Food mostly disappears within a week.

steps.title: Maintain conditions
steps.body: Keep bin 55–80°F, avoid flooding. Add bedding as volume decreases.

steps.title: Harvest castings
steps.body: When bin contents are dark and fine, harvest castings using light-separation or side-feeding method.

methods.name: Bokashi Fermentation (Kitchen-to-Soil)
methods.summary: Ferment food scraps (including some meats/dairy if tightly sealed) using bokashi bran, then bury to finish.
safety: Keep bucket sealed; bury pre-compost to avoid attracting pests.

steps.title: Load scraps + bran
steps.body: Add a layer of scraps, sprinkle bokashi bran, press down to remove air. Repeat.
cues: Contents compacted; minimal air pockets.

steps.title: Drain leachate (optional)
steps.body: Drain every 2–3 days. Dilute before using as a soil drench.

steps.title: Ferment sealed
steps.body: Keep sealed 2–3 weeks. It should smell tangy/pickled, not rotten.
cues: White yeast-like growth is normal; black/green mold is not.

steps.title: Bury to finish
steps.body: Bury in soil/trench or add to compost. Cover well; wait 2+ weeks before planting in that spot.

methods.name: KNF Fermented Plant Juice (FPJ)
methods.summary: Make a plant-based ferment (traditionally Korean Natural Farming) to feed plants with gentle, living inputs.
safety: Use clean jars; label; keep away from kids/pets.

steps.title: Harvest plant material
steps.body: Choose fast-growing plants (comfrey, nettle, grasses). Chop loosely.
cues: Material is fresh and not diseased.

steps.title: Mix with sugar
steps.body: Mix 1:1 by weight with brown sugar. Pack into jar leaving headspace.
cues: Sugar coats plant material thoroughly.

steps.title: Ferment
steps.body: Cover with cloth/paper and band. Store cool and shaded 5–7 days.

steps.title: Strain and store
steps.body: Strain liquid, bottle and label. Dilute before use (e.g., 1:500–1:1000).
cues: Liquid smells sweet/fermented, not putrid.

methods.name: JADAM-Style Natural Soap Spray (Mild Insect Pressure)
methods.summary: Prepare a mild, low-cost natural soap solution for leaf-surface pests (test on small area first).

supplies.name: Potassium soap or mild castile soap

safety: Always test on a few leaves first; avoid spraying in full sun or high heat.

steps.title: Mix test batch
steps.body: Mix a small batch at low concentration. Label the sprayer.
cues: Solution is well mixed; no clumps.

steps.title: Spot test
steps.body: Spray a small section of the plant. Wait 24 hours and check for burn.

steps.title: Apply at dusk/dawn
steps.body: Spray leaf undersides and suspected pest areas. Repeat as needed after rain.
cues: Coverage is even; beneficial insects avoided.

methods.name: Biochar: Make + Charge + Apply
methods.summary: Create biochar safely, then 'charge' it with nutrients/microbes before adding to soil.
safety: Fire hazard: follow local burn rules; keep water nearby; wear PPE.

steps.title: Select a safe burn setup
steps.body: Choose a safe, permitted location. Prepare water and tools.

checks.label: Burn is permitted and safe setup is ready

steps.title: Pyrolyze to charcoal
steps.body: Burn with limited oxygen (top-lit or retort) to make charcoal, not ash.
cues: Pieces are charred black but not crumbling to white ash.

steps.title: Quench and cool
steps.body: Stop the burn and quench with water. Allow to cool fully.

steps.title: Crush to size
steps.body: Crush to pea-size or smaller for best soil integration.

steps.title: Charge biochar
steps.body: Soak in compost tea, diluted urine, or mix into compost for 1–2 weeks before applying.
cues: Biochar is moist and smells earthy (not smoky).

methods.name: No-Till Bed Prep (Sheet Mulch)
methods.summary: Establish a new bed without digging by layering organic materials to suppress weeds and feed soil.

steps.title: Define bed and mow/flatten
steps.body: Mark bed edges. Mow low or flatten existing vegetation.
cues: Bed outline is marked.

steps.title: Lay weed barrier
steps.body: Overlap cardboard/newspaper. Wet it thoroughly to conform to soil.
cues: No gaps; edges overlap.

steps.title: Add compost layer
steps.body: Add 1–3 inches compost on top.
cues: Compost layer is even.

steps.title: Mulch heavily
steps.body: Add 3–6 inches of mulch. Water lightly if dry.
cues: Soil surface fully covered.

steps.title: Planting approach
steps.body: For transplants, pull mulch aside and plant into compost. For seeds, add a narrow strip of compost and sow there.

checks.label: Ready to plant or rest the bed

variants.name: Lasagna Garden
notes: Alternate compostable layers (greens/browns) for deeper build; allow time to settle.

methods.name: Raised Bed Setup (Basic)
methods.summary: Assemble a raised bed, fill with soil mix, and prepare for planting.
safety: Use gloves and eye protection when cutting/drilling.

steps.title: Choose location and orientation
steps.body: Pick a level spot with adequate sun. Orient long edge east-west when possible for even light.

steps.title: Assemble frame
steps.body: Build/assemble frame and square corners.

steps.title: Prep base
steps.body: Remove sod if needed or lay cardboard. Ensure drainage.

steps.title: Fill bed
steps.body: Fill with soil mix. Water to settle and top off.

steps.title: Mulch and mark zones
steps.body: Add light mulch and mark planting zones in SSA.

methods.name: Hugelkultur Mound Bed
methods.summary: Build a mound bed with buried wood to retain water and slowly feed soil over time (Central/Eastern European tradition).
safety: Avoid treated lumber; watch for sharp branches and lifting injuries.

steps.title: Lay the wood core
steps.body: Place logs/branches along the bed footprint. Soak if very dry.
cues: Wood layer is stable and stacked.

steps.title: Add nitrogen-rich layer
steps.body: Add greens/compost/manure to help decomposition.
cues: Wood is covered by nitrogen layer.

steps.title: Cover with soil
steps.body: Add topsoil and compost to form a mound. Shape with gentle slopes.

steps.title: Mulch and plant
steps.body: Mulch well. Plant drought-tolerant on top, moisture lovers near base.
cues: Mound is covered and stable.

methods.name: Keyhole Garden (Compost Basket Bed)
methods.summary: Build a circular raised bed with a central compost basket for efficient watering and fertility (Southern African popularization).

steps.title: Mark circle + keyhole access
steps.body: Mark a circle (6–8 ft) and a wedge path to the center.

steps.title: Build outer wall
steps.body: Stack stones/bricks to desired height. Ensure stability.

steps.title: Install compost basket
steps.body: Place perforated basket in the center for kitchen scraps and water infiltration.

steps.title: Fill and layer
steps.body: Fill bed with soil/compost. Optionally add drainage layer at bottom for wet climates.

steps.title: Plant and mulch
steps.body: Plant intensively; mulch. Feed/water through basket regularly.
cues: Central basket is accessible from the keyhole path.

methods.name: Square Foot Gardening Layout
methods.summary: Plan intensive spacing using a 1-foot grid to maximize harvest in small areas.

steps.title: Measure bed and create grid
steps.body: Divide bed into 1-foot squares using string or slats.
cues: Grid is visible and labeled.

steps.title: Assign crops per square
steps.body: Use spacing rules (e.g., 1 tomato, 4 lettuce, 9 beets). Account for trellises.
cues: Each square has a crop assignment.

steps.title: Schedule successions
steps.body: Plan follow-on plantings for each square after harvest windows.
cues: Successions recorded with dates/temperature cues.

methods.name: Three Sisters Polyculture (Corn • Beans • Squash)
methods.summary: Indigenous polyculture system where corn supports beans and squash shades soil and suppresses weeds.

steps.title: Prepare mounds or blocks
steps.body: Create mounds or block rows. Amend with compost in planting spots.
cues: Mounds are formed and enriched.

steps.title: Plant corn first
steps.body: Plant corn in clusters/blocks for pollination. Wait until it is ~6 inches tall.

steps.title: Plant beans
steps.body: Plant beans around corn stalks once corn is established.
cues: Beans placed 3–4 inches from stalks.

steps.title: Plant squash
steps.body: Plant squash around the mound edges to sprawl and shade soil.
cues: Squash has room to spread.

steps.title: Maintain
steps.body: Weed lightly early; then let squash canopy suppress. Monitor pests and water during dry spells.

variants.name: Milpa Variant
notes: Add additional companion crops (chilies, amaranth, tomatillo) based on climate and tradition.

methods.name: Milpa System (Diverse Maize Field Garden)
methods.summary: A diverse field-garden polyculture combining maize, legumes, squash, and supportive crops for resilience.

steps.title: Select crop mix for your climate
steps.body: Choose 3–8 crops that mature well in your season length. Record the mix in SSA.

steps.title: Prepare plot with organic matter
steps.body: Add compost/manure in bands or mounds; avoid over-tilling if possible.

steps.title: Stagger planting windows
steps.body: Plant tall support crops first, then climbers, then ground covers. Adjust to frost and rains.

steps.title: Observe and adapt
steps.body: Weekly scouting: light weeding, pest checks, moisture checks, and re-seeding gaps.

methods.name: Zai Pits (Water Harvesting Planting Pits)
methods.summary: Dig small pits to capture water and concentrate compost/manure—used in Sahel restoration farming.

steps.title: Mark pit grid
steps.body: Mark pits spaced based on crop (commonly 2–3 ft apart).
cues: Grid is marked and consistent.

steps.title: Dig pits and berm
steps.body: Dig pits and mound soil downhill as a micro-berm to slow runoff.

steps.title: Add organic matter
steps.body: Add compost/manure into pits; cover lightly with soil.

steps.title: Plant with first rains/irrigation
steps.body: Plant seeds/transplants when moisture is available. Mulch around pits.
cues: Moisture collected in pits; mulch in place.

methods.name: Waffle Gardens (Sunken Grid Beds)
methods.summary: Create sunken square cells that capture water and protect plants from wind—used by Indigenous peoples in arid regions.

steps.title: Lay out grid
steps.body: Mark a grid of squares (e.g., 12–18 inches).
cues: Grid lines are marked.

steps.title: Excavate cells
steps.body: Dig down each square and pile soil into raised berms between cells.

steps.title: Amend and plant
steps.body: Add compost to each cell and plant. Water fills cells and infiltrates slowly.
cues: Cells hold water briefly without washing out.

steps.title: Mulch berms
steps.body: Mulch berms to reduce erosion and evaporation.

methods.name: Slope Terracing (Erosion Control)
methods.summary: Create simple terraces or swales on slopes to slow water, reduce erosion, and increase infiltration.
safety: Avoid working on unstable slopes after heavy rains.

steps.title: Map contour lines
steps.body: Use an A-frame level or laser to mark true contour lines across the slope.
cues: Contour lines are level end-to-end.

steps.title: Cut and shape terrace/swale
steps.body: Excavate along contour; place soil on downhill side to form a berm.

steps.title: Stabilize
steps.body: Plant groundcovers, add mulch, and use rocks/logs where needed to prevent slumping.

steps.title: Overflow strategy
steps.body: Plan safe overflow routes for extreme rain events (spillways). Record in SSA notes.

methods.name: Drip Irrigation Setup + Seasonal Tune
methods.summary: Install drip lines, test flow, and tune schedule based on crop stage and weather.
safety: Check local backflow requirements when connecting to potable water.

steps.title: Plan zones
steps.body: Group beds by water needs. Measure run lengths and emitter spacing.
cues: Zones match crop water needs.

steps.title: Install header + filter/regulator
steps.body: Install mainline with filter and pressure regulator. Flush line before adding emitters.

steps.title: Lay drip lines
steps.body: Lay emitter lines along rows, anchor with staples. Add end caps.

steps.title: Test and fix leaks
steps.body: Run system. Check for leaks, clogs, uneven output. Adjust as needed.

steps.title: Set baseline schedule
steps.body: Set a starting schedule; adjust weekly based on soil moisture and plant cues.

methods.name: Rainwater Harvesting (Barrels + Overflow)
methods.summary: Collect roof runoff into barrels/cisterns and safely route overflow away from foundations.
safety: Use screens to reduce mosquitoes; do not drink without proper treatment.

steps.title: Choose stable platform
steps.body: Place barrel on level, strong platform near downspout. Ensure easy access.

steps.title: Install diverter and screen
steps.body: Cut/fit diverter. Add inlet screen.

steps.title: Set overflow
steps.body: Route overflow away from house; connect to garden swale or drainage path.
cues: Overflow won’t undermine foundation.

steps.title: Test in light rain or hose
steps.body: Verify flow into barrel and through overflow.

methods.name: Seed Starting Indoors (Tray Method)
methods.summary: Start seedlings indoors with consistent moisture/light, then harden off for transplant.

steps.title: Prep trays and labels
steps.body: Fill trays with moistened seed-start mix. Label variety and date.
cues: Mix is evenly moist; labels placed.

steps.title: Sow seeds
steps.body: Sow at recommended depth. Gently press and mist.

steps.title: Maintain humidity and warmth
steps.body: Cover until germination. Bottom-water if possible to prevent damping-off.

steps.title: Provide strong light
steps.body: Once sprouted, provide 14–16 hrs light. Keep lights close to prevent legginess.

steps.title: Pot up and harden off
steps.body: Pot up if rootbound. Harden off outdoors for 7–10 days before transplant.

methods.name: Direct Seeding (Row/Block)
methods.summary: Sow seeds directly into prepared soil with good seed-to-soil contact and moisture management.

steps.title: Prepare seedbed
steps.body: Rake smooth, remove clods, lightly firm. Create furrows or block marks.
cues: Surface is fine-textured and level.

steps.title: Sow and cover
steps.body: Sow at proper depth and spacing. Cover lightly and firm for contact.
cues: Seeds are covered evenly.

steps.title: Water gently
steps.body: Water with a fine spray to avoid washing seeds away. Keep evenly moist until germination.

steps.title: Thin and weed
steps.body: Thin to final spacing. Weed while small to reduce competition.

methods.name: Transplanting (Low-Stress Technique)
methods.summary: Transplant seedlings with minimal root shock and proper watering.

steps.title: Water seedlings
steps.body: Water seedlings 1–2 hours before transplanting.

steps.title: Prepare holes
steps.body: Dig holes slightly larger than root ball. Add compost if needed.
cues: Holes spaced and ready.

steps.title: Plant at correct depth
steps.body: Handle by leaves, not stems. Plant at same depth (or deeper for tomatoes). Firm gently.
cues: Plants upright; root ball fully covered.

steps.title: Water in
steps.body: Water thoroughly to remove air pockets. Mulch once soil is warm.
cues: Soil settled; no dry pockets.

methods.name: Mulching (Moisture + Weed Control)
methods.summary: Apply mulch to reduce evaporation, suppress weeds, and moderate soil temperatures.

steps.title: Weed and water
steps.body: Weed lightly and water the bed before mulching.
cues: Bed is weeded; soil moist.

steps.title: Apply mulch depth
steps.body: Apply 2–4 inches around plants (keep mulch off stems).
cues: Mulch is even; stems not buried.

steps.title: Maintain
steps.body: Top up mulch as it decomposes or thins.

methods.name: Cover Cropping (Soil Building)
methods.summary: Plant cover crops to protect soil, add biomass, fix nitrogen (legumes), and suppress weeds.

steps.title: Select mix
steps.body: Choose species for goals (nitrogen, biomass, erosion control) and season window.

steps.title: Prepare and seed
steps.body: Lightly rake soil, broadcast seed, rake in, and water.

steps.title: Manage growth
steps.body: Monitor establishment. Spot reseed thin areas if needed.

steps.title: Terminate and mulch-in-place
steps.body: Cut/roll at appropriate stage. Leave residues as mulch or incorporate lightly if needed.

methods.name: Crop Rotation Plan (3–4 Year)
methods.summary: Create a rotation plan to reduce pest/disease pressure and balance soil nutrients.

steps.title: Group crops by family
steps.body: Group plants by botanical family (solanaceae, brassicas, legumes, cucurbits, etc.).
cues: Crop families listed in SSA.

steps.title: Assign families to beds by year
steps.body: Rotate families so the same family doesn’t return to a bed for 3+ years where possible.
cues: Each bed has a family assignment for next season.

steps.title: Add notes for heavy feeders
steps.body: Note heavy feeders and add compost accordingly; plan legumes ahead of heavy feeders.
cues: Amendment needs are recorded per bed.

methods.name: Companion Planting Layout
methods.summary: Plan supportive plant pairings for pest confusion, pollinator support, and space efficiency.

steps.title: Pick your target crops
steps.body: List main crops and known issues (aphids, squash vine borer, etc.).
cues: Main crops and pressures listed.

steps.title: Choose companions
steps.body: Add flowers/herbs (marigold, basil, dill, nasturtium) and trap crops where appropriate.
cues: Companions selected with purpose notes.

steps.title: Lay out spacing and timing
steps.body: Ensure companions don’t shade or crowd. Stagger sowing if needed.
cues: Layout respects mature size and access paths.

methods.name: Integrated Pest Management: Scout → Identify → Respond
methods.summary: Weekly garden scouting with a tiered response plan: cultural controls, physical removal, biological support, then low-toxicity sprays if needed.

steps.title: Walk each bed systematically
steps.body: Check undersides of leaves, stems, and soil surface. Note damage patterns.
cues: You’ve inspected at least 5 plants per bed.

steps.title: Identify likely cause
steps.body: Match symptoms to pests/disease/nutrient issues using your reference notes.
cues: A likely cause is recorded (or marked 'unknown').

steps.title: Apply least-invasive control
steps.body: Start with hand removal, pruning, improved airflow, watering changes, or row cover. Escalate only if needed.
cues: Chosen control logged with date.

steps.title: Follow-up check
steps.body: Recheck in 3–7 days and note improvement or escalation.

methods.name: Row Cover / Low Tunnel Setup
methods.summary: Install row cover to protect from pests, wind, and light frost.

steps.title: Measure and place hoops
steps.body: Space hoops 3–4 ft apart for stability.
cues: Hoops are secure and evenly spaced.

steps.title: Drape and secure fabric
steps.body: Drape fabric over hoops. Secure edges with weights/soil to seal gaps.
cues: Edges are sealed; fabric not shredding in wind.

steps.title: Vent as needed
steps.body: Open ends on warm days to prevent overheating.

methods.name: Cold Frame / Mini Greenhouse Management
methods.summary: Use a cold frame for season extension: venting, watering, and hardening off.

steps.title: Set up temperature monitoring
steps.body: Place a thermometer inside. Note daily high/low trends.
cues: You can see or record inside temperature.

steps.title: Vent daily
steps.body: Vent on sunny days to prevent overheating. Close before evening chill.

steps.title: Water lightly
steps.body: Water when top layer dries; avoid waterlogging.

methods.name: Succession Planting Schedule
methods.summary: Plan staggered plantings for continuous harvest and steady kitchen supply.

steps.title: Choose crops that benefit
steps.body: Select crops like lettuce, radish, beans, carrots, cilantro.
cues: You have a list of succession crops.

steps.title: Set intervals
steps.body: Choose intervals (7–21 days depending on crop and heat). Record reminders in SSA.
cues: Intervals assigned per crop.

steps.title: Track and adjust
steps.body: Note germination and harvest windows; adjust future sowings for heat/frost.

methods.name: Pruning & Training (Tomatoes/Vines/Fruit)
methods.summary: Improve airflow and productivity with appropriate pruning and training.
safety: Sanitize tools between diseased plants to reduce spread.

steps.title: Identify what to prune
steps.body: Remove dead/diseased foliage first. For tomatoes, decide single/double leader and remove suckers accordingly.
cues: You know your training goal per plant.

steps.title: Prune in dry weather
steps.body: Prune when foliage is dry to reduce disease spread. Avoid heavy pruning in extreme heat.

steps.title: Tie and support
steps.body: Tie stems gently. Check ties monthly for girdling.

steps.title: Dispose of diseased material
steps.body: Bag and remove diseased material (do not compost if severe).

methods.name: Harvest + Weigh + Log
methods.summary: Standard harvest flow: pick, clean, weigh, and log yields for planning and inventory sync.

steps.title: Harvest at peak
steps.body: Harvest during cool parts of day when possible. Handle gently to prevent bruising.
cues: Produce looks firm and undamaged.

steps.title: Clean and sort
steps.body: Brush dirt, rinse if needed, and sort by quality (fresh use vs processing).
cues: Sorted into 'eat now' vs 'process/preserve'.

steps.title: Weigh and record
steps.body: Weigh each crop batch and log to SSA harvest tracker (crop, bed, date, weight).
cues: Weights recorded with units.

steps.title: Route to next step
steps.body: If preserving, create a preservation task. If storing, add inventory/storehouse entries with location.

methods.name: Seed Saving (Basic)
methods.summary: Save viable seeds from healthy plants; dry, label, and store for next season.
safety: Avoid saving seed from diseased plants; ensure complete drying to prevent mold.

steps.title: Select healthy parent plants
steps.body: Choose vigorous, disease-free plants. Note variety and traits.
cues: Parent plants selected and recorded.

steps.title: Collect mature seed
steps.body: Wait for full maturity (dry pods or fully ripe fruit depending on crop).
cues: Seed is fully developed; pods/fruits mature.

steps.title: Clean and dry
steps.body: Separate seed from chaff/pulp. Dry thoroughly in shade with airflow.
cues: Seed snaps (not bends) and shows no moisture.

steps.title: Label and store
steps.body: Label variety, date, and source. Store cool/dry/dark. Log batch in SSA.
cues: Seed packets labeled and stored.

methods.name: Hydroponics: Kratky (No-Pump)
methods.summary: Simple passive hydroponics method for greens using a nutrient reservoir and net cups.
safety: Keep nutrients out of reach of children/pets; avoid light leaks to prevent algae.

steps.title: Prepare reservoir
steps.body: Fill with mixed nutrient solution. Ensure container is opaque or covered.
cues: No light leaks; solution mixed.

steps.title: Set seedlings in net cups
steps.body: Place seedlings in inert medium. Roots should reach solution or wick moisture until they do.
cues: Seedlings stable; roots oriented downward.

steps.title: Maintain air gap
steps.body: As water level drops, an air gap forms for roots. Do not keep fully topped off.

steps.title: Monitor growth and nutrients
steps.body: Check for algae, odor, and nutrient depletion. Refresh solution as needed.
